definition: A function that calls itself with a smaller subproblem until reaching a base case
A way to describe how an algorithm's runtime grows as input size increases
An ordered collection of elements accessible by numeric index in constant time
A sequence of nodes where each node holds a value and a reference to the next
A data structure mapping keys to values via a hash function for near-constant lookup
An algorithm that halves the search space each step by comparing to the middle element
A LIFO (last-in-first-out) collection with push and pop operations
A FIFO (first-in-first-out) collection with enqueue and dequeue operations
A variable that stores the memory address of another value
A measure of how an algorithm's runtime scales with the size of its input
